Examples: * https://github.com/Expensify/App/actions/runs/22966296197/job/66670667766 * https://github.com/Expensify/App/actions/runs/22969068092/job/66680550745 ``` Summary of all failing tests FAIL tests/ui/SessionTest.tsx (199.161 s) ● Deep linking › should not remember the report path of the last deep link login after signing out and in again thrown: "Exceeded timeout of 120000 ms for a test. Add a timeout value to this test to increase the timeout, if this is a long-running test. See https://jestjs.io/docs/api#testname-fn-timeout." 110 | }); 111 | > 112 | it('should not remember the report path of the last deep link login after signing out and in again', async () => { | ^ 113 | expect(hasAuthToken()).toBe(false); 114 | 115 | const url = getInitialURL(); at it (tests/ui/SessionTest.tsx:112:5) at Object.describe (tests/ui/SessionTest.tsx:55:1) ● Deep linking › should not reuse the last deep link and log in again when signing out Can't access .root on unmounted test renderer =============================== Coverage summary =============================== Statements : 15.99% ( 20101/125644 ) Branches : 11.95% ( 13298/111270 ) Functions : 11.83% ( 3984/33666 ) Lines : 16.34% ( 19344/118372 ) ================================================================================ 160 | // This overrides the previous sign-in. 161 | Linking.setInitialURL(url); > 162 | const {unmount: unmount2} = render(); | ^ 163 | 164 | await waitForBatchedUpdatesWithAct(); 165 | at Object.get [as root] (node_modules/react-test-renderer/cjs/react-test-renderer.development.js:14707:19) at root (node_modules/@testing-library/react-native/src/render.tsx:102:29) at buildRenderResult (node_modules/@testing-library/react-native/src/render.tsx:70:10) at renderInternal (node_modules/@testing-library/react-native/src/render.tsx:44:10) at Object. (tests/ui/SessionTest.tsx:162:43) at asyncGeneratorStep (node_modules/@babel/runtime/helpers/asyncToGenerator.js:3:17) at _next (node_modules/@babel/runtime/helpers/asyncToGenerator.js:17:9) ```

title: [$250] Distance expense map preview crops start/finish points

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Description In distance expenses, the start and finish points of the route are not visible in the map preview on the expense details page. The map image is rendered in landscape orientation but the receipt preview area is portrait, causing the route endpoints to be cropped/cut off depending on origin/destination. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to a workspace chat 2. Create a track distance map expense 3. Open the expense details page 4. Observe the map preview — the start and/or finish points may not be visible ## Expected Behavior The full route should be visible in the map preview, including start and finish points. ## Actual Behavior The start and finish points are not visible in the map preview because the map image aspect ratio doesn't match the preview container. ## Proposed Solution Display the distance map receipt image like any other receipt (using the 1:1 source map image) instead of giving it special treatment with a landscape crop. The existing receipt display styles should already handle this correctly — we just need to remove whatever special-case handling causes the map to be displayed differently. ## References - Originated from [Web-Expensify PR \#50555 comment](https://github.com/Expensify/Web-Expensify/pull/50555#issuecomment-3950387580) ## Platform Web, Mobile
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @dukenv0307

title: [Personal card import] Missing reimbursable toggle on personal card expenses

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Issue reported by:** @sakluger **Slack conversation**: [#migrate](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07NMDKEFMH/p1773159772643849) ## Action Performed: 1. Import a new personal card in OldDot or NewDot. 2. Go to one of the expenses in NewDot. ## Expected Result: You should see the toggle for whether it's reimbursable, just like in classic. It should default to the card-level selection in effect at the time the transaction was imported. This setting for personal card expenses is not affected by the workspace rule that determines whether cash expenses are set to be reimbursable. ## Actual Result: There's no reimbursable toggle for personal card expenses ## Workaround: This can be changed in OldDot ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.35-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/folder/13176908/test-cases/41237444 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** testexpensify9+kdii@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Android 16, Samsung A15. **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: Preconditions: - The user has a workspace. - QBO UK is connected in accounting. 1. Open the app. 2. Go to Workspaces > {select the WS with QBO UK connected} > Accounting > Export > Export out-of-pocket expenses > Export as > Vendor bill ## Expected Result: When selecting Vendor Bill in Export as, the error message should be displayed clearly and fully visible to allow the user tap on X to close it. ## Actual Result: After selecting Vendor Bill in Export as, the error message is partially hidden/overlapped by the OS navigation buttons. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0f3ca8e5-f61f-47be-8feb-420db89e4c8e [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.35-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-8317/folder/13176677/41236545/1241613428 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** biruknew45sw@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Web, android **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: 1. Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com/ 2. Create a new user (Without +) 3. Select the purpose "Track and budget expenses." 4. Create a workspace in onboarding and finish the flow 5. Open the Admin room ## Expected Result: According to the TC, the Onboarding tasks and free trial badge should appear in the Admin room when using email without "+" ## Actual Result: Even when using an email without ‘+’, the onboarding task and free trial badge do not appear in the admin room. Instead, they appear in the concierge chat. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cda89499-74a2-461f-9e80-ac639ef2e4c4 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
